tools: one set of {@item artisan's tools|phb} of your choice.

default: (a) {@item chain mail|PHB} or (b) {@item leather armor|PHB}, a {@item longbow|PHB}, {@item Arrows (20)|PHB|20 arrows}
(a) {@filter martial weapon|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=martial weapon} and {@item shield|PHB} or (b) two {@filter martial weapons|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=martial weapon}
(a) {@item light crossbow|PHB} and {@item Crossbow Bolts (20)|PHB|20 bolts} or (b) two {@item handaxe|phb|handaxes}
(a) a {@item dungeoneer's pack|PHB} or (b) an {@item explorer's pack|PHB}

subclass.name: Master at Arms
subclassFeatures: Master at Arms|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Master at Arms|LLAF|3
Master of Forms|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Master at Arms|LLAF|7

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: At 1st level, you learn one {@filter Fighting Style|optionalfeatures|feature type=fs:af} of your choice from the list at the end of this class description. To learn a Fighting Style, you must meet any prerequisites it has, and you cannot learn the same Fighting Style more than once.

At certain levels in this class, you learn additional Fighting Styles, as shown in the Fighting Style column in the Fighter class table above. Whenever you gain a level, you can replace one Fighting Style you know with another of your choice.

classFeature.name: Second Wind
entries: You can fight on where lesser warriors would fail. Also at 1st level, you can use your bonus action to regain hit points equal to {@dice 1d10} + your Fighter level. Once you do so, you must finish a short or long rest before you can do so again.
Upon reaching 14th level, you can use your Second Wind bonus action twice between each short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Martial Exploits
entries: At 2nd level, you begin to learn techniques that enhance your martial skill in and out of battle. You gain the features below:

entries.name: Exploits Known
entries: You know two {@filter Exploits|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:ME} of your choice from the list at the end of this class. To learn an Exploit you must meet any prerequisites it has.
You learn additional Martial Exploits of your choice as you gain levels in this class, as shown in the Exploits Known column.
When you gain a level, you can replace an Exploit you know with another you could learn.

entries.name: Saving Throws
entries: If one of your Exploits requires a creature to make a saving throw, the Exploit saving throw DC is calculated based on your Strength or Dexterity. Once you choose which ability score to use, it cannot be changed:

classFeature.name: Exploit Dice
entries: The Fighter table shows the number of Exploit Dice you have to perform any Exploits you know. Most Exploits require you to expend Exploit Dice to use them. You can only use one Exploit per weapon attack, ability check, or saving throw, and you regain expended Exploit Dice when you finish a short or long rest.
Your Exploit Dice begin as {@dice d6}s and increase in size as you gain levels in this class, as shown in the Exploit Die column of the Fighter table.
When adding an Exploit Die to a roll, always roll your Exploit Die and add it to the result.

classFeature.name: Tactical Skill
entries: Dedicated training has also honed your skills. Whenever you make an ability check with a tool or skill you are proficient in, you can expend an Exploit Die and add it to your roll. You can do so after you roll the d20, but before you know the outcome of your ability check, success or failure.

classFeature.name: Eye for Talent
entries: Upon reaching at 3rd level, your training allows you to quickly evaluate the combat abilities of others. As a bonus action, you can take the {@action Search} action targeting one creature you can see wihtin 30 feet (DC equals 8 + its Challenge Rating).

If you have hit that creature with a weapon attack witin the last minute, you can add your Fighter level to your roll. On a success, you learn one characteristic of your choice about the target, chosen from the following table:

rows: One Trait or Action from its stat block

entries: On a failure, you do not learn anything about the creature, and you cannot use this feature on that creature again until you finish a long rest, or you hit it with a weapon attack.

classFeature.name: Warrior Archetype
entries: At 3rd level, choose the Warrior Archetype from the list here that best represents the training and tactics of your Fighter:
Your Archetype grants you features at 3rd level, and again at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level in this class. If an Archetype feature forces a saving throw it uses your Exploit save DC.

entries.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: Some Archetypes have a list of Archetype Exploits that you learn at the Fighter levels noted in the Archetype description. These Exploits do not count against your number of Exploits Known and cannot be switched out for other Exploits. If you do not meet their prerequisites, you learn them regardless.

classFeature.name: Archetype Feature
entries: At 7th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warrior Archetype.

classFeature.name: Archetype Feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warrior Archetype.

classFeature.name: Archetype Feature
entries: At 15th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warrior Archetype.

classFeature.name: Archetype Feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warrior Archetype.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 14th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn. Moreover, if you use your action to {@action Dash} or to {@action Disengage} you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action on that turn.
When you reach certain levels in this class, the number of attacks you can make as part of your Attack action increases; at 11th level (3 attacks) and at 17th level (4 attacks).

classFeature.name: Extra Attack (2)
entries: At 11th level, you can attack three times whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack (3)
entries: At 17th level, you can attack four times whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Action Surge
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can push yourself past your limits, if only for a moment. Once during your turn, you can choose to take one additional action. After you do so, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use this feature again.
When you reach 20th level, you can use this feature twice between each short or long rest, but only once per turn.

classFeature.name: Action Surge (2)
entries: When you reach 20th level, you can use Action Surge twice between each short or long rest, but only once per turn.

classFeature.name: Indomitable
entries: Your fighting spirit allows you to grasp success from the jaws of defeat. Beginning at 9th level, when you fail a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead. Once you use this feature you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.
At 13th level, you can use this feature twice per long rest, and at 17th level you can use it three times per long rest.

classFeature.name: Martial Superiority
entries: The chaos and thrill of combat fuels your fighting spirit. Starting at 11th level, once per round in combat you can use one 1st-degree or 2nd-degree Martial Exploit you know without expending an Exploit Die.

classFeature.name: Indomitable (2)
entries: At 13th level, you can use Indomitable twice between long rests.

classFeature.name: Indomitable (3)
entries: At 17th level, you can use Indomitable three times between long rests.

classFeature.name: Relentless
entries: At 20th level, your combat skill rivals the great heroes of legend. Each time you roll initiative, you regain all expended Exploit Dice.
Finally, you can use any 3rd-degree Martial Exploits you know without limit. You still must expend Exploit Dice as normal.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Knight
entries: Arcane Knights supplement their skill with the armaments of war with arcane spells. Compared a mage who studies solely magic, the Arcane Knight can only produce minor spells, but, combined with their deadly skill with the weapons of warfare, these minor spells become potent enhancements.

subclassFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have learned to cast arcane spells that augment your abilities in combat. You gain the following features:

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn two Cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Arcane Knight spell list|spells|subclass=Alternate Fighter: Arcane Knight (LLAF)|level=0} at the end of this Archetype, and you learn an additional Arcane Knight Cantrip at 10th level.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st-Level and Higher
entries: You know three {@filter 1st-level Arcane Knight spells|spells|subclass=Alternate Fighter: Arcane Knight (LLAF)} from the list on the next page. The Spells Known column of the Arcane Knight Spellcasting table shows when you learn more spells, all of which must be a level for which you have spell slots.
When you gain a level, you can replace one of your Spells Known with another spell from the Arcane Knight spell list.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Arcane Knight Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast the spells you know. To cast a spell, you must expend a spell slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all spell slots when you finish a long rest.
For example, if you know the 1st-level spell {@spell magic missile} and have a 1st-level and a 2nd-level spell slot available, you can cast magic missile using either slot.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Arcane Knight spells. So, you use Intelligence whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. You also use your Intelligence modifier when setting the saving throw DC or making a spell attack roll for an Arcane Knight spell.

subclassFeature.name: Enchanted Armaments
entries: You magically bond yourself to your armaments of war. Over the course of 1-hour, which can be during a short or long rest, you can perform a ritual to bond with a weapon or shield that you are touching. You can have two such objects bound. If you perform this ritual again, you must release one object.

So long as they are on the same plane of existence as you, you can use your bonus action to conjure one or both objects, instantly donning them. You can use either of these objects as a spellcasting focus, and you cannot be disarmed of them.

subclassFeature.name: War Magic
entries: You can seamlessly weave minor spells with weapon attacks. Whenever you use your action to {@action Cast a Spell} you can make a single weapon attack as a bonus action on that turn.

subclassFeature.name: Enchanted Strikes
entries: Your weapons are enchanted to undercut defenses against your spells. Whenever you hit a creature with an {@subclassFeature Enchanted Armament|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Arcane Knight|LLAF|3} attack, the creature has disadvantage on the first saving throw you force it to make against one of your Arcane Knight spells before the end of your next turn.
Also, when you hit a target with an Enchanted Armament attack, you can change the damage type of your weapon to match the damage type of an Arcane Knight spell you know.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Surge
entries: Drawing upon your stamina triggers a burst of magic. Each time you use {@classFeature Action Surge|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|6}, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can choose to teleport before or after the additional action from your Action Surge.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Arcane Knight
entries: You expertly weave spell and sword together. When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can cast an Arcane Knight spell with a casting time of one action in place of one attack.
You can still use any Arcane Knight spells you know with a casting time of one attack (such as {@spell lightning lure (alternate)|llspell|lightning lure}) as normal.

subclassFeature.name: Champion
entries: Champions forgo all other forms of training to focus on their raw physical might. These immense figures strive to maintain peak physical condition through relentless dedication. In battle, Champions perform supernatural feats of athleticism and dominate foes with raw power.

subclassFeature.name: Champion Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known, and cannot be switched out for other Exploits when you gain a Fighter level:

subclassFeature.name: Mighty Warrior
entries: The raw physical might you have cultivated enhances your attacks. Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.
At 15th level, this critical hit range increases again and your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a d20 roll of 18 through 20 on the d20.

subclassFeature.name: Peak Athlete
entries: You are a paragon of athleticism. You gain proficiency in {@skill Athletics}, and you gain a climbing speed and swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
If you were already proficient in Athletics, you gain proficiency in another Fighter class skill.

subclassFeature.name: Remarkable Strength
entries: Your physical abilities are unrivaled by normal mortals. When you make a Strength or Constitution ability check or saving throw, you can use {@optfeature feat of strength|LLExploits} or {@optfeature heroic fortitude|LLExploits} without expending an Exploit Die.
Used this way, feat of strength counts as if you spent one Exploit Die, though you can add Exploit Dice to it.

subclassFeature.name: Devastating Critical
entries: Whenever you score a critical hit with a weapon attack you deal bonus damage equal to your Fighter level.
At 15th level, when you score a critical hit with a weapon attack, you can maximize the damage instead of rolling. Once you do you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Champion
entries: You are a nearly perfect physical specimen and have become exceedingly hard to kill. If you begin your turn with less than half of your hit points remaining, but at least 1 hit point, you regain hit points equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Captain
entries: Not all Fighters rely solely on themselves in battle, some lead others with their knowledge of tactics. Captains lead from the front, issuing orders and inspiring greatness in their allies by their own brave deeds. By their presence, a true Captain can transform even an unorganized militia into a deadly force.

subclassFeature.name: Captain Exploits
entries: You learn certain Tactical Exploits from the {@loader Warlord Class|class/LaserLlama; Warlord.json} at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. These Exploits do not count against your total number of Exploits Known:
Tactical Exploits from this feature use the following rules:

entries.name: Replacing these Exploits
entries: Each time you gain a level in this class, you can replace one Tactical Exploit you learned through this feature with another Tactical Exploit of the same degree. If a Tactical Exploit has a Warlord level prerequisite, you can learn it if your Fighter level meets that prerequisite.

entries.name: Leadership Modifier
entries: Some Tactical Exploits require a Leadership modifier. Choose your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma as your Leadership modifier for these Exploits.

entries.name: Leadership save DC
entries: When you force a creature to make a saving throw to resist one of your Tactical Exploits, you use your Leadership modifier to calculate the Save DC:

subclassFeature.name: Student of War
entries: You have studied strategies of both politics and war. You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: {@skill Deception}, {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, or {@skill Persuasion}.
Whenever you make an ability check with the chosen skill you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Strategic Command
entries: Your determination in the face of danger inspires your allies to fight on. Whenever you use {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1}, you can choose up to three other creatures within 30 feet of you who can see or hear you to also regain hit points equal to your Leadership modifier (minimum of 1) + your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Surge
entries: When you use {@classFeature Action Surge|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|6}, you can choose another creature within 30 feet that can see or hear you. It can use its reaction to move up to its speed without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack||opportunity attacks} and then make a single weapon attack.

subclassFeature.name: Inspiring Commands
entries: Once per turn when you use a Tactical Exploit that targets at least one friendly creature, one target of that Exploit gains temporary hit points equal to your Leadership modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Captain
entries: Your bravery and tactical ability rank you amongst the most successful military leaders of the age. At the end of a long rest, you can replace any number of your Captain Exploits with Tactical Exploits of the same degree.
Finally, whenever you use {@subclassFeature Heroic Surge|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Captain|LLAF|10}, you can target up to two creatures within range.

subclassFeature.name: Marksman
entries: While all fighters learn to draw a bow or hurl a javelin, those who train as Marksmen dedicate themselves to mastering ranged weapons of all types. Often, their deadly skills are backed up with an unmistakable swagger and unshakable confidence. Relying on their innate talents and signature grit, there are few challenges a true Marksman cannot overcome.

entries.name: Gunpowder & Firearms
entries: If your game includes firearms and gunpowder, and your Marksman has been exposed to the operation of such weapons, they are proficient with them.

subclassFeature.name: Marksman Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Elite Reflexes
entries: Your reaction times are enhanced by your talent and training. Whenever you are forced to make a Dexterity check or saving throw, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Deadly Focus
entries: You can quiet your body to fire with deadly accuracy. When you begin your turn and are not {@condition surprised} or {@condition incapacitated}, you can choose to enter a state of Focus, which imposes the following benefits and effects until the end of your turn:

items: Your speed is reduced to 0 feet.
Until you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack, you have advantage on all ranged weapon attack rolls.
When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a ranged weapon, you can re-roll the die. You must use this new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2.

subclassFeature.name: Quick Shot
entries: You react to danger without delay. You add your Proficiency Bonus to initiative rolls so long as you are not {@condition Incapacitated}.
Moreover, when you roll initiative and are not {@condition Surprised}, you can use your reaction to make one ranged weapon attack before any other creatures have a chance to act in combat.

subclassFeature.name: Tactical Reposition
entries: Your talents and training allow you to move about in battle to setup the perfect shot. Whenever you use your {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1}, you gain the benefits of the {@action Disengage} action and your speed increases by 10 feet until the end of your current turn.

subclassFeature.name: Reliable Shot
entries: You make even impossible shots with ease. Your normal and long range for ranged weapon attacks increases by a number of feet equal to 10 times your Fighter level.
Also, once per turn when you have advantage on a ranged weapon attack, you can forgo your advantage to instead make one additional ranged weapon attack as part of that action.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Marksman
entries: Your marksmanship rivals the great deities and demigods of the hunt. When you use {@subclassFeature Deadly Focus|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Marksman|LLAF|3}, your speed is reduced to 10 feet instead of 0, and you have advantage on all ranged weapon attack rolls while your Focus lasts.

subclassFeature.name: Master at Arms
entries: While most Fighters master one specific martial discipline, a Master at Arms is the rare warrior who is able to truly master multiple styles of combat. Whether by their talent, dedication, or extraordinary skills, these elite Fighters learn all they can about the theories of combat. A Master at Arms is always on the lookout for a new weapon or style of fighting to perfect.

entries.subclassFeature: Student of Battle|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Master at Arms|LLAF|3|LLAF

subclassFeature.name: Student of Battle
entries: Your ability to learn and execute martial techniques exceeds that of most other warriors, and even other trained Fighters. You gain the following benefits to your Fighter features:

entries.name: Exploits
entries: You learn two {@filter 1st-degree Martial Exploits|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:1ME} of your choice from the list at the end of this class. These Exploits do not count against your total number of Exploits Known.

You learn additional Martial Exploits following the same rules at certain levels: at 5th level you learn two {@filter 2nd-degree Exploits|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:2ME}, and at 9th level you learn one {@filter 3rd-degree Exploit|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:3ME}.

entries.name: Exploit Dice
entries: Your total number of Exploit Dice increases by 1, and all of your Exploit Dice increase to become {@dice d8}s.
At certain Fighter levels your Exploit Dice increase again; at 5th level they are {@dice d10}s, and at 11th level they are {@dice d12}s.

subclassFeature.name: Advanced Technique
entries: Your ability to learn and execute combat styles exceeds that of most other warriors and even other trained Fighters. You learn an additional Fighting Style of your choice, but it does not count against your number of Fighting Styles Known.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Theorist
entries: You study the theory of combat as a sage studies their tomes. You gain proficiency in {@skill History}, and whenever you make an Intelligence ({@skill History}) check related to the history of combat, warfare, or armaments of war, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Forms
entries: Your skill allows you to learn exotic techniques from a variety of disciplines. You learn two Exploits of your choice from any class's Exploit list (see Advanced Martial Classes below).

If these Exploits have a level prerequisite, you can learn it so long as your Fighter level meets its level prerequisite. You must still meet all other prerequisites it might have. Exploits you learn with this feature don't count against your total number of Exploits Known.
You learn another Exploit of your choice following these same rules at 15th level and 18th level.

entries.name: Advanced Martial Classes
entries: Including the Alternate Fighter class here, there are currently six classes that make use of Exploits:

items: Alternate Fighter & Fighter: Expanded
{@loader Alternate Barbarian & Barbarian: Expanded|class/LaserLlama; Alternate Barbarian.json}
{@loader Alternate Rogue & Rogue: Expanded|class/LaserLlama; Alternate Rogue.json}
{@loader Warlord Class & Warlord: Expanded|class/LaserLlama; Warlord.json}
{@link Brawler Class|https://www.patreon.com/posts/brawler-class-v2-95658650} - {@i Patreon Exclusive}
{@link Wanderer Class|https://www.patreon.com/posts/wanderer-class-0-101633003} - {@i Patreon Exclusive}

subclassFeature.name: Masterful Focus
entries: You channel the rush of battle to rejuvenate your technical skills. Whenever you use your {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1} or {@classFeature Action Surge|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|6}, you also regain one of your expended Exploit Dice.

subclassFeature.name: Warrior Savant
entries: Your knowledge of combat techniques allows you to modify your skill set. During each short or long rest, you can practice combat drills and replace one Exploit you know with another Martial Exploit you meet the prerequisites for.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Superiority
entries: Your technical mastery of combat is near-supernatural. You regain one of your expended Exploit Dice at the start of each of your turns in combat, so long as you are not {@condition Incapacitated}.

subclassFeature.name: Sylvan Archer
entries: Originally hailing from the elder forests of the world, Fighters who train to become Sylvan Archers learn to enchant arrows with elder elvish magic. Traditionally, these warriors served as guardians for places of great primal power, but they have been known to train worthy outsiders in this ancient magic.

subclassFeature.name: Sylvan Lore
entries: You are learned in the Sylvan arts. You learn to speak, read, and write {@language Sylvan}, the ancient language of forests. You also gain proficiency in {@skill Nature}. You can make Wisdom ({@skill Nature}) checks in place of the normal Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) checks.
Finally, you learn the {@spell druidcraft} cantrip, and Wisdom is your spellcasting modifier for it. When you cast druidcraft you can use a piece of ammunition as a spellcasting focus.

subclassFeature.name: Enchanted Shots
entries: You have learned to enchant arrows and other ammunition with elder Sylvan magic. You gain the benefits listed below:

entries.name: Shots Known
entries: You learn two {@filter Enchanted Shots|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:ASA} of your choice from the list at the end of this Archetype description.
You learn one additional Enchanted Shot of your choice when you reach 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level in this class.
When you gain a level in this class you can replace one of the Shots you know with another Shot of your choice.

entries.name: Enchanting a Shot
entries: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack, you can magically apply the effects of one Enchanted Shot you know as part of that attack.

You can use a number of Enchanted Shots equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. If you have no uses left, you can expend an Exploit Die to use one of your Shots again.

entries.name: Saving Throws
entries: If one of your Enchanted Shots forces a saving throw or ability check, it uses your Exploit save DC.

subclassFeature.name: Sylvan Archer Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Sylvan Shot
entries: You weave minor amounts of magic into every shot you take. Your attacks with ranged weapons gain the benefits below:

items: They are magic for overcoming resistance and immunity.
If you miss, you can use a bonus action to curve the shot and repeat your attack against another target in range.
When a creature succeeds on a saving throw against an Enchanted Shot, it still takes half as much damage.

subclassFeature.name: Enchanted Quiver
entries: Whenever you make a ranged weapon attack you can conjure a piece of magical ammunition as part of the attack. After the attack, hit or miss, this ammunition vanishes.
Also, when you roll initiative, you regain one expended use of your Enchanted Shot feature.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Sylvan Archer
entries: The power of your enchantments rivals that of the ancient elvish guardians. Whenever you use an Enchanted Shot it deals additional damage equal to your Exploit Die

Also, you can overload one of your Enchanted Shots with overwhelming magic. When you use an Enchanted Shot you can empower it, causing creatures of your choice within 20 feet of the target to also suffer the effects of the Shot.
Once you empower an Enchanted Shot this way you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

subclassFeature.name: Knight Errant
entries: Often raised at court or in the upper circles of the nobility, the archetypal Knight Errant is a chivalric warrior of the highest order. They excel at mounted combat and wander the world in search of adventure and opportunities for heroism. These paragons of virtue are bound to defend the weak and weary.

subclassFeature.name: Knight Errant Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Chivalric Mark
entries: You draw the ire of your foes to protect your comrades. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can Mark it until the end of your next turn. While Marked, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you, so long as you remain within 10 feet of it.
If a Marked cretaure deals damage to a target other than you, you can use your reaction to make one melee weapon attack against your Mark.

subclassFeature.name: Courtly Pedigree
entries: You have been tutored in the traditional skills of nobility and the chivalric virtues. You learn to speak, read, and write one additional language, and you gain proficiency in one of the following: {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, or {@skill Persuasion}.

subclassFeature.name: Trained Knight
entries: You been trained to ride a saddle from birth. You cannot be knocked from a mount against your will, and you can mount or dismount a trained mount with 5 feet of movement.

subclassFeature.name: Noble Guardian
entries: You are a shield for the meek. When you or a creature within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to interpose your melee weapon or shield, granting it a bonus to its Armor Class against that attack equal to your Exploit Die.
If the attack still hits, you can expend one Exploit Die and grant the target resistance to the damage of that attack.

subclassFeature.name: Unyielding Knight
entries: No one can threaten your allies while you stand. You apply a {@subclassFeature Chivalric Mark|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Knight Errant|LLAF|3} to any creature you hit with a melee weapon, and Marked creatures have disadvantage on attacks against creatures other than you while you are within 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Perilous Charge
entries: You run down your foes, mounted or on foot. Once per turn, when you move 10 feet in a straight line then hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your Exploit save DC or it takes bonus damage equal to your Exploit Die and is knocked {@condition prone}. If you are mounted, it has disadvantage on its save.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Knight Errant
entries: You will defend your allies against any foe. You gain a special reaction that you can take once during every turn, but you can only use this special reaction to make an {@action opportunity attack}, to attack a creature under {@subclassFeature Chivalric Mark|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Knight Errant|LLAF|3}, or to use {@subclassFeature Noble Guardian|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Knight Errant|LLAF|7} to shield yourself or another creature.

subclassFeature.name: Ronin
entries: Though they have been known by many names over the ages, Ronin are Fighters who have been expelled from the service of their lord or land. Regardless of the reason, these highly skilled warriors now roam the world seeking absolution through battle. With nothing to lose, these warriors fight with a deadly ferocity and ruthless focus unlike any other.

subclassFeature.name: Ronin Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Exiled Courtier
entries: You were once an influential member of a noble court or an important advisor. You learn to speak, read, and write one additional language of your choice, and you gain proficiency in one of the following skills: {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}.

subclassFeature.name: Unyielding Spirit
entries: You fight with the unrivaled focus and ruthless ferocity of a warrior with nothing left to lose. As a bonus action, you can focus yourself to gain advantage on all weapon attack rolls and ignore any levels of {@condition Exhaustion} you have until the end of your current turn. When you use this bonus action you also gain temporary hit points equal to your Fighter level.
You can use this feature once per short or long rest with no adverse effects. However, each additional time you use it, you suffer 1 level of Exhaustion at the end of that turn.

subclassFeature.name: Lordly Bearing
entries: Your experience and former station shine through no matter how downtrodden you may become. Whenever you make an ability check with your {@subclassFeature Exiled Courtier|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Ronin|LLAF|3} skill, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.
You also gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you are already proficient in Wisdom saving throws, you instead gain proficiency in Charisma or Intelligence saving throws.

subclassFeature.name: Unrelenting
entries: You can push through adversity that would defeat a lesser warrior. When you roll initiative with no uses of {@subclassFeature Unyielding Spirit|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Ronin|LLAF|3} remaining, you regain one expended use.
Moreover, whenever you finish a short rest, you current level of {@condition Exhaustion}, if any, is reduced by 1.

subclassFeature.name: Swift Strikes
entries: You rain blows down upon foes like the raindrops of a storm. Once per turn when you have advantage on a weapon attack roll against a creature, you can forgo your advantage to make one additional weapon attack against that creature.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Ronin
entries: Your battle focus is so absolute that you can avoid the grasp of death itself, if only for a short time. When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can immediately take an extra turn.

During this extra turn, you remain at 0 hit points, and any damage you take causes you death saving throw failures as normal. At the end of this extra turn, you immediately fall to the ground {@condition unconscious} with 0 hit points, and if you gained three death saving throw failures during that turn, you die.

Once you use this feature you must complete a long rest before you can use it again. If you have no uses left, you can expend a use of {@classFeature Action Surge|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|6} to use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Shadowdancer
entries: The energies of the Shadowfell grant many strange and dark abilities to those who can wield them. The Fighters known as Shadowdancers learn to draw these sinister powers through themselves to create an exact copy of their bodies in combat.

subclassFeature.name: Shadowdancer Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Conjure Shade
entries: As a bonus action, you can draw on the umbral magic of the Shadowfell to conjure a Shade in an unoccupied space you can see within 15 feet. This Shade is a magical copy of you created from Shadows. It uses the {@creature Shade|LLAF} stat block below. Though it resembles you, it has no personality of its own. In combat, your Shade uses the following rules:
In combat, your Shade uses the following rules:

entries.name: Combat
entries: The Shade shares your turn in combat, but it cannot act on its own. You can telepathically command it to move up to its speed on your turn, but it is destroyed if it is more then 30 feet away from you at the end of your turn.

entries.name: Attacking
entries: Whenever you take the {@action Attack} action, you can choose for each individual attack to originate from you, or a Shade. If it originates from your Shade, you make the attack as if you were in its space, with all of your normal benefits.

entries.name: Duration
entries: Your Shade lasts until it is destroyed, you use a bonus action to dismiss it, or you conjure another Shade.

subclassFeature.name: Dance of Shadows
entries: You and your Shade dance about in a blur of shadows. While your Shade is within 30 feet, you can use an action to switch places with it without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack||opportunity attacks}.
At 7th level, you can make a single attack from either you or the Shade after you use this feature. At 15th level, you can use this feature with either your action or your bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Transfer Consciousness
entries: As an action, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1} to transfer your consciousness into your Shade. For 10 minutes, your body is {@condition Unconscious} as you sense and interact with the world through this Shade. For this duration, this Shade can move up to 1 mile from your body without being destroyed.
This effect ends if the Shade is destroyed or you use your action to end it. You then return to your body as normal.

subclassFeature.name: Dark Sacrifice
entries: When a creature within 10 feet of a Shade is hit by an attack, you can use a reaction to cause your Shade to intercept that attack, reducing the damage the creature would take by your Fighter level, but destroying your Shade in the process.

subclassFeature.name: Restorative Shadows
entries: You draw the power of your destroyed Shades back into you. When one of your Shades is destroyed by taking damage, you can gain temporary hit points equal to two Exploit Dice.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Shadowdancer
entries: Your command over the Shadow Magic you use is unrivaled. Whenever you conjure a Shade, you can choose to conjure two Shades, without expending any additional resources.

subclassFeature.name: Runecarver
entries: Before the mortal races rose to power, the world was ruled by giants and their powerful Rune magic. Traces of this ancient magic can still be found in the wild and remote places of the world, and Fighters who learn to use the power of Runes to augment combat ability become known as Runecarvers.

subclassFeature.name: Rune Carving
entries: You have learned the ancient art of carving Runes. You learn to speak, read, and write {@language Giant}, the language of your Runes, and you gain proficiency with {@item mason's tools|PHB}. Your knowledge of Runes also grants you the benefits below:

entries.name: Runes Known
entries: You learn two {@filter Runes|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:RNA} of your choice from the list at the end of this Archetype description. Some Runes have a Fighter level prerequisite. You can learn these Runes at the same time that you meet all of their prerequisites.
You learn one additional Rune of your choice when you reach 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level in this class.
When you gain a level in this class you can replace one of the Runes you know with another Rune of your choice.

entries.name: Inscribing a Rune
entries: Over the course of 1 hour, which can be during a short or long rest, you can use a set of mason's tools to inscribe one Rune you know into one weapon, suit of armor, shield, or another object that you can wear or hold. A creature wearing or wielding that object gains the benefits of that Rune.
Each Rune you know can only be inscribed in one object. Inscribing that Rune into another object dispels any of your previous inscriptions of that Rune.

entries.name: Invoking a Rune
entries: While you are wearing or wielding an object inscribed with a Rune you can Invoke its effect. Once invoked, a Rune cannot be Invoked again until you complete a short or long rest. Only you can Invoke your Runes.

entries.name: Runic Exploits
entries: Exploits learned from Runes don't count against Exploits Known, use your Exploit Die, and can be used once per turn without expending an Exploit Die.

entries.name: Saving Throws
entries: If a Rune requires a creature to make a saving throw, it does so against your Exploit save DC.

subclassFeature.name: Runic Might
entries: As a bonus action, you can fill yourself with Runic magic, gaining the benefits here for 1 minute:

items: You and anything you are wearing grows to become Large in size, so long as there is room.
You have advantage on all Strength ability checks and saving throws.
Once per turn when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack you gain a bonus to your damage roll equal to your Exploit Die.

entries: This feature ends early if you use a bonus action to revert to your normal size, or if you are {@condition incapacitated}.
You can use this feature once per long rest. When you have no uses of this feature left, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1} to use Runic Might again.

subclassFeature.name: Runic Ward
entries: You can shield your allies with the stalwart magic of Runes. When a target you can see within 30 feet is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to add your Constitution modifier (minimum of +1) to its Armor Class against that attack.
You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
If you have no uses left, you can Invoke one of your Runes to use this feature again in place of the Rune's normal effect.

subclassFeature.name: Unyielding Fortitude
entries: Your body has been fortified by the elder magic you wield. You have advantage on saving throw to resist being moved against your will, {@condition Poisoned}, {@condition Stunned}, or knocked {@condition prone}.

subclassFeature.name: Elder Insight
entries: Your understanding of the ancient magic of Runes has grown deeper. When you have no uses of a Rune remaining you can expend one Exploit Die to Invoke that Rune again.
You can only Invoke each Rune you know one additional time in between each short or long rest in this way.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Runecarver
entries: You can draw upon the elder power of your Runes to match the stature of the ancient giants. When you use {@subclassFeature Runic Might|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Runecarver|LLAF|3}, you can grow to become Huge in size, so long is there is room. While you are Huge, your reach increases by 5 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Crusader
entries: Many warriors swear Oaths to gods of battle and war, but not all are blessed with the divine power of Paladins or Clerics. Those who fight for the gods without their explicit blessing are called Crusaders. These zealots are champions of divine causes, their fervent belief fueling their battle fury.

subclassFeature.name: Crusader Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Crusader's Ire
entries: You mark your foes for judgment. As a bonus action, you can Mark a creature you can see within 60 feet as the target of your Crusader's Ire, granting you the following benefits:

items: Once per turn when you miss the creature with a weapon attack, you can immediately make another weapon attack against that creature using the same weapon.
When the creature is within your reach and casts a spell or makes an attack against a creature other than you, you can make an {@action opportunity attack} against it as a reaction.
Whenever it forces you to make a saving throw, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

entries: Your Mark lasts for 1 minute, until the creature is slain, or until you use this feature to Mark another creature. You can use this feature once per short or long rest as no cost, after which, you must expend one Exploit Die to use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Fanatical Disciple
entries: You are an obsessive follower of your faith, learning all you can. You gain proficiency in {@skill Religion}. Whenever you make a {@skill Religion} check related to your god or cause, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Renewed Fervor
entries: Your fanaticism grants you bursts of fervor in battle. When you use {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1} you regain the use of {@subclassFeature Crusader's Ire|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Crusader|LLAF|3}.
Also, when you Mark a creature with Crusader's Ire, you can move up to 30 feet toward it as part of the same bonus action without expending your movement.

subclassFeature.name: Zealous Fury
entries: Your conviction allows you to survive blows that would slay those of lesser faith. When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead and immediately make one weapon attack against your attacker.
You can use this feature once per short or long rest with no negative effects. When you have no uses left you can use this feature again, but you instantly gain one level of {@condition Exhaustion}.

subclassFeature.name: Righteous Judgment
entries: You are the arbiter of divine wrath. When you hit the target of {@subclassFeature Crusader's Ire|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Crusader|LLAF|3} with a weapon attack, you can end the Mark to have your attack to deal maximum damage instead of rolling.
If the attack reduces the creature to 0 hit points you regain the use of Crusader's Ire. Once you use this feature you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Crusader
entries: When the target of {@subclassFeature Crusader's Ire|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Crusader|LLAF|3} targets you with an attack, you can use your reaction to make a single weapon attack against that creature. If you use this reaction after the attack hits you, your weapon attack is made with advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Guardian
entries: Guardians are elite defensive warriors whose strengths shine while fighting side by side with their allies. Though trained to use weapons of all types, Guardians are especially effective when using a protective style of fighting with a weapon and shield. Alone, a Guardian is a small threat, but when fighting alongside their allies they are impenetrable walls of steel.

subclassFeature.name: Guardian Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Warrior Smith
entries: You have learned to keep your armor in peak condition. You gain proficiency in {@item smith's tools|PHB}. Whenever you make an ability check that uses smith's tools you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

Also, during the course of a long rest, you can spend 1 hour using your smith's tools to improve one set of armor or a shield you touch. It grants its user +1 bonus to Armor Class until the beginning of your next long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Iron Guardian
entries: You have trained to defend others, no matter the personal cost. You gain one of the following Fighting Styles of your choice: {@optfeature Defensive Fighting|LLStyles}, {@optfeature Protection|LLStyles}, or {@optfeature Shield Warrior|LLStyles}. It doesn't count against your number of Fighting Styles, and can only be replaced by a Style from the list above.

Also, if you are wielding a shield and a creature within 5 feet of you it hit by an attack, you can use {@optfeature reposition|LLExploits} as a reaction. You can choose to do so without expending an Exploit Die, but no temporary hit points are gained.
When you use this reaction, you only take the damage of the triggering attack if it would have also hit you.

subclassFeature.name: Inspiring Sentinel
entries: You presence inspires others to stand their ground. When you use {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1}, creatures of your choice within 5 feet of you gain temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).

Moreover, when you use your {@subclassFeature Iron Guardian|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Guardian|LLAF|3} reaction, {@optfeature reposition|LLExploits} grants temporary hit points even if you do not expend an Exploit Die as part of the reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Stalwart Defender
entries: You are strongest when standing alongside your allies. You can now use {@optfeature reposition|LLExploits} on creatures within 10 feet of you, including with {@subclassFeature Iron Guardian|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Guardian|LLAF|3}, so long as there is a clear path to that creature. Using reposition does not end {@optfeature defensive stance|LLExploits} or {@optfeature hold the line|LLExploits} for you.

subclassFeature.name: Adamant Guardian
entries: You improve your defensive technique to better defend your allies. You can now use {@optfeature reposition|LLExploits} on creatures within 30 feet of you, including with {@subclassFeature Iron Guardian|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Guardian|LLAF|3}, so long as there is a clear path for you to move to the creature.
You are also no longer limited to using {@optfeature daring rescue} once between each short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Guardian
entries: You are a legendary shield for those who fight beside you. You gain a special reaction that you can take once per turn, but it can only be used for {@subclassFeature Iron Guardian|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Guardian|LLAF|3}, your Guardian Exploits, or {@action Opportunity Attack||opportunity attacks}.

subclassFeature.name: Guerrilla
entries: No matter the mission, a Guerrilla will execute it with utmost perfection. Sometimes known as commandos, these warriors are marked by their determination and adaptability. Whether harsh terrain, vicious monsters, enemy soldiers, or powerful spellcasters, nothing short of death will cause the willpower of a Guerrilla warrior to waver in the pursuit of their goals.

subclassFeature.name: Adaptable Combatant
entries: You have honed your skills to meet any challenge. You gain proficiency in {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Perception}, {@skill Stealth}, or {@skill Survival}. If you choose a skill you are proficient in, you instead gain a bonus to checks with that skill equal to your Exploit Die.

Moreover, during the course of a long rest, you can spend 1 hour performing combat drills and practicing techniques. At the end of this hour, you can replace any number of your current Exploits Known with other Martial Exploits of your choice, so long as you meet the new Exploit prerequisites.

subclassFeature.name: Guerrilla Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and cannot be switched for other Exploits:

subclassFeature.name: Survivalist
entries: You are an expert at overcoming natural obstacles. You learn either the {@optfeature Mariner|LLStyles} or {@optfeature Mountaineer|LLStyles} Fighting Style. It does not count against your total number of Fighting Styles, but you cannot replace it with another Fighting Style.

subclassFeature.name: By Land or Sea
entries: You have trained to find success in any environment, either it be land, sea, or sky. You gain the benefits listed below:

items: At the end of each long rest you can change switch your Fighting Style from {@subclassFeature Survivalist|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Guerrilla|LLAF|3} for the other option.
You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour.
You ignore the effects of nonmagical {@quickref difficult terrain||3}.
Whenever you use your {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1} you also gain the benefits of the {@action Dash} action or the {@optfeature mighty leap|LLExploits} Exploit.

subclassFeature.name: Identify Weakness
entries: You pinpoint your enemy's weaknesses and ruthlessly punish them. When you succeed on an {@classFeature Eye for Talent|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|3} check against a creature you can forgo the normal knowledge you would gain, and instead, the next time you force it to make a saving throw against one of your Exploits it does so with disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: Unwavering
entries: You are remarkably hardy, even amongst elite warriors. Each time you use {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1} you regain the maximum amount of hit points and you regain one expended Exploit Die.
You can also expend your {@classFeature Action Surge|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|6} to instantly replace one of your Exploits with another Exploit you could learn.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Guerrilla
entries: There is nothing that will stand between you and your goals when they are within your reach. When you roll initiative and are not {@condition Surprised}, you gain all of the following benefits:

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Fighter level.
You can take the {@action Search} action to use {@classFeature Eye for Talent|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|3}.
You can immediately move up to your full speed without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack||opportunity attacks}.

subclassFeature.name: Hound Master
entries: Since the dawn of civilization, animals have worked alongside mortals. Most notable of the domesticated animals is the dog. The earliest hunters worked in tandem with these loyal beasts, sharing both food and fire. Some Fighters still take up this mantle, training Loyal Hounds to adventure by their side.

entries.name: Loyal Hounds & other Canines
entries: Depending on your table and game setting, there are many creatures that could be a Loyal Hound.
In a more mundane or low-magic setting, your Hound is likely going to be a dog or wolf. In other more fantastical worlds, any four-legged beast or monstrosity could serve you as a Loyal Hound.

subclassFeature.name: Hound Master Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Loyal Hound
entries: When you adopt this Archetype, you complete the training of a Loyal Hound. Your Hound is friendly to you and your allies and obeys your commands. It uses the {@creature Loyal Hound|LLAF:E} stat block, which uses your Proficiency Bonus (PB) and Exploit save DC. Your Loyal Hound uses the following rules:

entries.name: Combat
entries: In combat, your Hound acts during your turn. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but it only takes the {@action Dodge} action unless you use a bonus action to order it to take an action from its stat block, or to take another action. Also, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can forgo one attack to order your Hound to make a single Bite attack.
If you are {@condition Incapacitated}, your Hound can act on its own and will defend you and itself to the best of its abilities.

entries.name: Dying
entries: If your Hound is reduced to 0 hit points, it makes death saving throws like a player character would. When the Hound is at 0 hit points, you can use an action to touch it and expend one Exploit Die to automatically stabilize it.

entries.name: Training a New Hound
entries: If your Hound dies, you can spend 8 hours training a replacement for your Loyal Hound, so long as a suitably canine-type creature can be found. Once trained, the creature uses the Loyal Hound stat block.

subclassFeature.name: Iron Jaws
entries: Your Loyal Hound is infused with a portion of your fighting spirit. The damgae of your Hound's Bite attack is considered to be magical for overcoming resistances and immunities.

subclassFeature.name: War Hound
entries: You train your Hound to use basic combat maneuvers. Over the course of 1 hour, which can be during a short or long rest, you teach your Loyal Hound one 1st-degree Exploit you know that it could reasonably use. It gains one Exploit Die equal to yours which it can expend to use its Exploits, and it regains all Exploit Dice after a short or long rest.
Your Loyal Hound can be trained to use two Exploits, but you can use this feature to replace one of its current Martial Exploits with another Exploit following the same guidelines.

subclassFeature.name: Steadfast Companion
entries: You can face any foe so long as your Hound is by your side. While you and your Loyal Hound are within 30 feet of each other you both gain a special reaction which you can use to grant the other advantage on a saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Canine Fury
entries: Once per turn when you order your Loyal Hound to make a Bite attack, it can make two Bite attacks instead of one.
In addition, you can use War Hound to teach your Loyal Hound 2nd-degree Exploits, it can be trained to use up to three Exploits, and it gains a second Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Hound of Legend
entries: Infused by mythic power of your bond, your Hound rivals the great beasts of legend and myth. Your Loyal Hound uses the {@creature Hound of Legend|LLAF:E} stat block in place of its previous stats.
Finally, when your Loyal Hound is slain, it reforms after 1 hour, appearing in an unoccupied space within 15 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Mystic
entries: Where most Fighters look to increase their physical abilities, those known as Mystics work to unlock the psionic potential of their mind. Drawing upon a wondrous inner power, these ascetic warriors can perform feats that would be impossible through strength alone. Where others strive toward physical perfection, Mystics strive for spiritual enlightenment.

entries.name: Unleash your Psionic Potential
entries: The Mystic Archetype presented here is a replacement for the Psi Knight publshed in Tasha's Cauldron of Everything. As the Eldritch Knight is to the wizard, so the Mystic Knight it to the {@link Psion|https://www.gmbinder.com/share/-MPkCSxSj0OETiEd3Pyf}.

subclassFeature.name: Psionics
entries: You have unlocked the true potential of your mind, and have learned to manifest psionic spells, much like a {@loader Psion|class/LaserLlama; Psion.json} does:

entries.name: Mental Limit
entries: Your Fighter level limits the potency of spells you can manifest with your psionics. This is shown in the Mental Limit column of the Mystic Psionics Table.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st-Level and Higher
entries: You learn two 1st-level spells of your choice from the Mystic spell list on the following page. The Spells Known column of the Mystic Psionics table shows when you learn more Mystic spells of 1st-level and up, but they cannot exceed your Mental Limit level.
Whenever you gain a Fighter level, you can pick one of the Psion spells that you know and replace it with another Psion spell of your choice, of a level equal to your Mental Limit or lower.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: Your mind is your spellcasting focus for your Psion spells. You must have a free hand to cast spells with somatic or material components, and you must provide material components consumed by the spell or with a required gold cost. When you manifest a spell with Psionics, you exhibit noticeable changes.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Psion spells. You use Intelligence when a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. You also use your Intelligence modifier when setting the saving throw DC or making a spell attack roll for a Psion spell you know.

subclassFeature.name: Psi Points
entries: The potential of your mind is represented by a pool of Psi Points. The Mystic Psionics table shows how many Psi Points you have to manifest your spells of 1st-level and higher. To manifest a spell, you expend Psi Points equal to the spell's level (0 for Cantrips). You regain all expended Psi Points when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Psionic Talents
entries: Your training has begun to unlock minor psionic abilities. You unlock two {@filter Mystic Talents|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:MT} of your choice from those available to the {@loader Psion Class|class/LaserLlama; Psion.json}. If a Talent has a prerequisite Psion level, you can learn it if your Fighter level meets that prerequisite.
You unlock an additional Mystic Talent of your choice when you reach 7th, 15th, and 18th level in this class.
If you learn a spell through a Mystic Talent, it is as a Mystic spell for you, using Intelligence as its spellcasting modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Phase Step
entries: When you use {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1}, you also gain the benefits of the {@action Dash} action and partially discorporate. Until the end of your current turn, you can move through solid nonmagical objects and creatures as if they were {@quickref difficult terrain||3}.
If you end your movement inside an object or creature, you are instantly shunted to the nearest unoccupied space, taking {@damage 1d10} force damage for every 5 feet you are forced to move.

subclassFeature.name: Inscrutable Mind
entries: Your psionic abilities make you difficult for others to control. You gain resistance to psychic damage, and whenever you are forced to make an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw you gain a bonus to the roll equal to your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Mental Ward
entries: You can project psionic power to shield others. As a bonus action, you can spend 5 Psi Points to project a psionic ward out to a 30-foot radius. For 1 minute, creatures of your choice in this area gain the benefits of {@subclassFeature Inscrutable Mind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Mystic|LLAF|10}.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Mystic
entries: You have unlocked the full potential of your mind. You learn the {@spell telekinesis} spell, but it does not count against your number of Mystic Spells Known.
You can manifest the telekinesis spell once between each long rest, without expending any Psi Points. After that, you can epend 5 Psi Points to manifest it again.

Finally, whenever you use {@subclassFeature Phase Step|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Mystic|LLAF|7}, you also gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed until the end of your current turn. If, you end your turn flying you fall.

subclassFeature.name: Quartermaster
entries: All successful adventurers know the value of teamwork, but none value it more than the Quartermaster. These supportive Fighters strive to help their allies reach their full potential in combat. Putting the needs of their companions before their own, Quartermasters keep their comrades in top condition.

subclassFeature.name: Down to Earth
entries: You gain proficiency in {@item cook's utensils|PHB}, {@filter land vehicles|items|type=!treasure;!renaissance;vehicle (land);!modern;!futuristic}, and in {@skill Animal Handling}. Whenever you make an ability check that uses any of these proficiencies, you a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Quartermaster Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Rations
entries: You have learned to prepare potent the foodstuffs known as {@filter Rations|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:QMR} which keep your allies in peak condition. Rations use the rules below:

entries.name: Preparing Rations
entries: During the course of each long rest, you can spend 1 hour with {@item cook's utensils|PHB} and prepare a number of Rations up to your Constitution modifier choosing from the list of Rations on the following page. If a Ration has a Fighter level prerequisite, you can prepare that Ration as soon as you meet its prerequisite.
The Rations you prepare lose all beneficial properties at the end of your next long rest.

entries.name: Consuming Rations
entries: While holding a Ration, a creature can use a bonus action to eat it or feed it to a creature within 5 feet. Consuming a Ration instantly grants you its benefits, and immediately ends any other Ration benefits.

entries.name: On the Fly
entries: As an action, you can expend one Exploit Die and use a set of cook's utensils to prepare one Ration of your choice. However, you cannot regain the Exploit Die you spent to prepare this Ration until that Ration is consumed.

subclassFeature.name: Dependable Ally
entries: You are always prepared to lend a helping hand to a comrade. When you take the {@action Attack} action, you can forgo any number of attacks to use one of the following in place of each attack:

items: Take the {@action Help} action targeting a creature within 10 feet.
Take the {@action Use an Object} action to feed a Ration, potion, or another consumable item to a creature you can touch.
Make an ability check using a {@subclassFeature Down to Earth|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Quartermaster|LLAF|3} proficiency.
Give one weapon, item, or shield to a creature you can touch. It instantly equips that item and stows one item.

subclassFeature.name: Quick Ration
entries: You have greatly improved the speed at which you prepare Rations. You can use a bonus action on your turn to create a Ration On the Fly, eating it or feeding it to a creature within 5 feet as part of the same bonus action. Rations you prepare in this way lose all beneficial properties after 1 minute.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Ever Ready
entries: You always have something ready to serve. When you roll initiative and are not {@condition Surprised}, you prepare one Ration of your choice without expending an Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Quartermaster
entries: Constant culinary experiments with your Rations have made your body supernaturally hardy. Your Constitution score, and maximum Constitution score, both increase by 2, and you are immune to the {@condition Poisoned} condition.
You can also be under the effects of two Rations at once. If you eat a third Ration, you chose which Ration effect ends.

subclassFeature.name: Swordsage
entries: Known by many names through the ages: blademaster, sword saint, Swordsage, these mythic warriors dedicate their life to the art of the sword. Legends say each master Swordsage will take one apprentice in their lifetime, teaching them the secret blade arts and entrusting them with their mystical legacy.

subclassFeature.name: Student of the Blade
entries: Your training has granted you skills to compliment swordplay. You gain proficiency in either {@skill Acrobatics} or {@skill Performance}, and you can use your Dexterity, in place of Charisma, for Performance checks that incorporate a sword.
Also, when you make a Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) or a Dexterity ({@skill Performance}) check you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Swordsage Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Battle Trance
entries: You can clear your mind to enter the mystical trance that gives Swordsages their reputation. So long as you are not wearing heavy armor or a shield, you can use your bonus action to enter a Battle Trance. This Trance lasts for up to 1 minute and grants you the following benefits:

items: You have advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks.
You can take the {@action Dash} action as a bonus action.
You gain a +1 bonus to your Armor Class.
Once per turn, you can use a Swordsage Exploit without expending an Exploit Die. If it requires an Exploit Die roll, you use a {@dice d4} in place of your Die.

entries: Your Battle Trance ends early if you are {@condition Incapacitated}, if you don a shield or heavy armor, or if you end it with a bonus action. You can enter a Battle Trance once per short or long rest at no cost, after which you must expend an Exploit Die to use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Mythic Reflexes
entries: Your reflexes have been honed mythic levels in pursuit of perfection. You gain Proficiency in Dexterity saving throws, and you add your Proficiency Bonus to initiative rolls.
Also, your {@subclassFeature Battle Trance|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Swordsage|LLAF:E|3} AC bonus becomes +2.

subclassFeature.name: Reactive Trance
entries: You slip into the Trance at a moments notice. When you roll initiative and are not {@condition Surprised} or {@condition Incapacitated}, you can enter your {@subclassFeature Battle Trance|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Swordsage|LLAF:E|3} without expending a use.

subclassFeature.name: Storm of Steel
entries: You are a blur of flashing steel when you enter your Trance. Whenever you take the {@action Dash} action in the {@subclassFeature Battle Trance|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Swordsage|LLAF:E|3} you also gain the benefits of the {@action Disengage} action for that turn.
In addition, whenever you roll Exploit Dice for an Exploit or another ability while you are in your Battle Trance, you can roll those Dice twice and choose which total to use.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Swordsage
entries: You have become a Swordsage worthy of your own legends. You can enter and dismiss your {@subclassFeature Battle Trance|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Swordsage|LLAF:E|3} at will, without expending a use of the feature or an Exploit Die.
Finally, whenever you take damage while you are in your Battle Trance, you can expend an Exploit Die to reduce any damage you would take by two Exploit Dice.

subclassFeature.name: Tinker
entries: Those who become Tinkers work for an unorthodox form of martial skill. Rather than practice combat techniques, they augment their abilities with mechanical weaponry. Always designing and testing new Schematics, Tinkers and their inventive arsenal are a deadly force on the battlefield.

subclassFeature.name: Analytical Mind
entries: You have spent as much time in workshops as you have at the training ground. You gain proficiency with {@item tinker's tools|PHB|tinker's} and {@item smith's tools|PHB}. If you are already proficient with these tools, you gain proficiency with a set of tools of your choice.
Whenever you make an ability check that uses these tools, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit die.

subclassFeature.name: Inventive Arsenal
entries: You have developed a cacophony of wondrous modifications for your weapons, shields, and armor, known as Schematics. All Schematics you know use the following rules:

entries.name: Schematics Known
entries: You know two {@filter Schematics|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:TKS} from the list on the next page. You must meet the Schematic's prerequisites in order to learn it.
You learn one additional Schematic of your choice when you reach 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level in this class.
When you gain a level, you can replace a Schematic you know with another Schematic of your choice.

entries.name: Modifying an Object
entries: During each long rest, you can spend 1 hour using {@item tinker's tools|PHB|tinker's} or {@item smith's tools|PHB} to touch a number of objects equal to the number of Schematics you know, modifying each object with the effects of one Schematic. An object can only bear one modification.

In order to modify an object it must meet the requirements in that Schematic's description. Schematic modifications last until the end of your next long rest, at which point the objects return to normal unless you modify them again.
Only you gain the benefits from these modifications, to any other creature, the objects function as normal objects.

entries.name: Saving Throw
entries: If a Schematic requires a saving throw, your Schematic save DC is calculated as follows:

subclassFeature.name: Increased Function
entries: The workings of your inventions are more intuitive to others. If you spent 10 minutes teaching a creature, it can wield the special benefits of any item modified by your Schematics.
Moreover, objects modified by your Schematics count as magic for overcoming resistances and immunities, and you can modify magic weapons and armor with Schematics.

subclassFeature.name: Inventive Synergy
entries: The intricacies of your inventions work together and improve upon one another. You can modify an object with two of your Schematics so long as the object being modified meets the prerequisites of both Schematics in question.

subclassFeature.name: Flexible Innovation
entries: Your inventive arsenal can be adjusted on the fly to meet the challenges at hand. During the course of a short rest, you can use {@item tinker's tools|PHB|tinker's} or {@item smith's tools|PHB} to transfer one of your Schematic modifications from one object to another that also meets the prerequisites of that Schematic.
If the Schematic modifies an object so that it has Charges, the number of remaining Charges is transferred to the object.
In addition, you can modify an object three Schematics, so long as it meets the prerequisites for all three Schematics.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Inventions
entries: A weapon modified by your Schematics gain a +1 bonus to its attack and damage rolls for each Schematic applied to it, and any set of armor modified by a Schematic gains a +1 bonus to its Armor Class for each of your Schematics applied to it.
Weapons and armor modified by your Schematics cannot gain a bonus greater than +3, regardless of any bonuses the item may have had before applying your Schematics.

subclassFeature.name: Pugilist
entries: Most often coming from the school of hard knocks, Fighters known as Pugilists learned to fight in the dark underbelly of society. Where others use tactics and practiced techniques, Pugilists rely on brute force and dirty tricks. To them, every fight is a fight for their life and they find victory at any cost.

subclassFeature.name: Contender
entries: Your body itself is a deadly weapon. You learn the {@optfeature Brawling|LLStyles} Fighting Style, and the damage die of your unarmed strikes increases to match the size of your Exploit Die.

If you already know this Fighting Style, you instead learn {@optfeature Improvised Fighting|LLStyles}. Regardless, the Fighting Style you gain here does not count against your number of Fighting Styles.

subclassFeature.name: Pugilist Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the fighter levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Iron Physique
entries: Your battle-hardened physique allows you to shrug off blows that would send a normal person reeling. You can use your Constitution, in place of Dexterity, when calculating your Armor Class in light and medium armor. Moreover, when you are not wearing armor, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier.
Lastly, whenever you take damage from a source you can see, you can use {@optfeature brace up|LLExploits} as a reaction, gaining the temporary hit points before you take any damage.

subclassFeature.name: Counter Punch
entries: You have learned to exploit every opening your foes give you. When a creature you can see misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to make an unarmed strike, {@action Shove}, {@action Grapple}, or improvised weapon attack against it.
If you use force a creature to make a saving throw as part of this reaction attack, it has disadvantage on its roll.

subclassFeature.name: Resolute Strikes
entries: The grit that only comes from a life on the streets fuels your fists. Your unarmed strikes and improvised weapon attacks count as magical for overcoming resistance and immunity.

subclassFeature.name: Evasive Footwork
entries: You have trained to evade your foes' strikes in combat. When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn and make at least one unarmed strike, {@action Grapple}, or {@action Shove}, you also gain the benefits of the {@action Disengage} action until the end of that turn.

subclassFeature.name: Diamond Physique
entries: You have learned to take hits that would be deadly to weaker warriors. When you use {@optfeature brace up|LLExploits} with your reaction, you also gain resistance to that instance of damage.

You also learn the {@optfeature unbreakable|LLExploits} Exploit, but it doesn't count against your total number of Exploits Known, and you cannot replace it with another Exploit when you gain a level.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Pugilist
entries: Your name is known in the underbelly of every city. You have advantage on saving throws to resist and end being {@condition Incapacitated}, {@condition Paralyzed}, or {@condition Stunned}.
Finally, whenever you score a critical hit with an unarmed strike or improvised weapon, the target falls {@condition prone} and is {@condition Stunned} until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Witchblade
