entries: Driven by an overwhelming desire to vanquish the great evils of the world, Witchblades are willing to give up anything. This path leads them to enter into bargains with Eldritch Powers, beings of otherworldly might. Witchblades offer a fraction of their soul and unending fealty in return for eldrtich abilities.

subclassFeature.name: Pact Magic
entries: The bargain you have struck with your Patron has granted you the ability to cast spells, much like a {@loader Warlock|class/LaserLlama; Alternate Warlock.json} does:

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn one Cantrip of your choice from the {@filter Witchblade Spell List|spells|subclass=Alternate Fighter: Witchblade (LLAF:E)|level=0}. Upon reaching 10th level in this class you learn one additional Witchblade Cantrip of your choice.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Witchblade Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have, and the level of those spell slots. All of your spell slots from this feature are the same level. To cast one of your Witchblade spells of 1st-level or higher, you must expend a spell slot. You regain all of your expended Pact Magic spell slots when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st-Level and Higher
entries: You learn two 1st-level spells of your choice from the {@filter Witchblade spell list|spells|subclass=Alternate Fighter: Witchblade (LLAF:E)}. The Spells Known column of the Witchblade Spellcasting table shows when you learn more spells of 1st-level or higher. A spell you choose must be of a level no higher than what's shown in the table's Slot Level column for your level.
Whenever you gain a level, you can choose one Witchblade spell you know and replace it with another Witchblade spell, which must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Witchblade spells. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability, when setting a spell saving throw DC, and when making a spell attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Sanguine Offering
entries: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can offer your own vitality to increase the damage. You take necrotic damage equal to your Exploit Die and deal bonus necrotic damage equal to two Exploit Die.
The necrotic damage you take can't be reduced in any way.

subclassFeature.name: War Magic
entries: You can seamlessly weave minor spells with weapon attacks. When you use your action to {@action Cast a Spell} you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action on that turn.

subclassFeature.name: Otherworldly Step
entries: Drawing on your eldritch power allows you to slip through cracks in reality. Whenever you cast a Witchblade spell with a casting time of one action or use your {@classFeature Second Wind|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|1}, you can teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 60 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Greater Offering
entries: You restore your vitality by inflicting pain on your foes. When you use {@subclassFeature Sanguine Offering|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Witchblade|LLAF|3}, you can gain temporary hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt to the creature. However, temporary hit points from this feature only last for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Witchblade
entries: You can offer the souls of your foes to your Eldritch Power in return for increased vitality. When a creature you can see within 30 feet is slain, you can use your reaction to teleport to an unoccupied space of within 5 feet of the slain creature, plunge your weapon into it, and gain temporary hit points equal to twice the slain creature's Challenge Rating.

optionalfeature.name: Beguiling Shot
entries: The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it takes psychic damage equal to two Dice and until the start of your next turn, it is {@condition Charmed} by a creature of your choice that it can see. This effect instantly ends if you or your allies do anything harmful or hostile toward it.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack
Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Bursting Shot
entries: The target and all creatures within 10 feet of it must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, they take your choice of cold, fire, or lighting damage equal to two Exploit Dice.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack
Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Enfeebling Shot
entries: The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes necrotic damage equal to two Exploit Dice, and for 1 minute its weapon attacks deal half damage.
The creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack
Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Grasping Shot
entries: The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes piercing damage equal to two Exploit Dice, and for 1 minute it is bound by thorns. For the duration, its speed is halved, and the first time it uses its speed to move on each turn it takes piercing damage equal to two Exploit Dice.
A creature can end this effect by using its action to make a successful Strength check against your Exploit save DC.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack
Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack
The first time the target moves on each turn it takes

optionalfeature.name: Piercing Shot
entries: The target, and creatures directly behind it in a straight line out to 30 feet must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they take force damage equal to two Exploit Dice.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack
Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Seeking Shot
entries: In place of an attack, you use this Enchanted Shot to cause a piece of ammunition you are holding to magically seek out a creature that you have seen within the last minute.

If that creature is within 120 feet and there is a clear path to it (it need not be straight), it must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes force damage equal to two Exploit Dice and you instantly learn its location.

activation.condition: In place of an attack
In place of an attack

optionalfeature.name: Umbral Shot
entries: The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, it takes psychic damage equal to two Exploit Dice, and for 1 minute it is {@condition Blinded} by visual hallucinations.
The creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack
Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Banishing Shot
entries: The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, it is banished to a harmless demiplane for 1 minute.
The creature can use its action to repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. When it ends, it reappears in the unoccupied space closest to the space that it left.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Severing Shot
entries: The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes force damage equal to two Exploit Dice, and it cannot cast spells for 1 minute.
The creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Technical Shot
entries: The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it suffers the effects of one Martial Exploit you know, even if the Exploit could normally only be used with a melee attack.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Transposing Shot
entries: The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, it takes force damage equal to two Exploit Dice, and you switch places with it without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack||opportunity attacks}.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack

optionalfeature.name: Cloud Rune
entries: This Rune imbues the cunning magic of Cloud Giants. This Rune's wielder knows the {@optfeature mighty leap|LLExploits} Exploit.

entries.name: Invoke
entries: When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet is targeted by an attack, you can use your reaction to Invoke this Rune to redirect the triggering attack at another target within its reach, other then itself.

activation.condition: When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet is targeted by an attack
When a target you can see within 30 feet is hit by an attack

optionalfeature.name: Fire Rune
entries: This Rune imbues the fury of Fire Giants. This Rune's wielder knows the {@optfeature imposing presence|LLExploits} Exploit.

entries.name: Invoke
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can Invoke this Rune to conjure restraints of molten metal around it. It takes bonus fire damage equal to twice your Exploit Die and must succeed on a Strength saving throw. On a failure, it is {@condition Restrained} for 1 minute.
While Restrained it takes fire damage equal to twice your Exploit Die at the start of each of its turns. As an action, it can repeat the saving throw, ending this effect on a success.

activation.condition: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack
When a target you can see within 30 feet is hit by an attack

optionalfeature.name: Frost Rune
entries: This Rune imbues the primeval instincts of the Frost Giants. This Rune's wielder knows the {@optfeature arresting strike|LLExploits} Exploit.

entries.name: Invoke
entries: As a bonus action, you can Invoke this Rune to empower your body. For 10 minutes, your speed increases by 10 feet, and you gain a bonus to all Strength and Constitution ability checks and saving throws equal to your Exploit Die.
You can Invoke this Rune as part of using {@subclassFeature Runic Might|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Runecarver|LLAF|3}.

activation.condition: When you hit a target with a weapon attack
When a target you can see within 30 feet is hit by an attack

optionalfeature.name: Hill Rune
entries: This Rune imbues the overwhelming might of the Hill Giants. This Rune's wielder knows the {@optfeature mighty thrust|LLExploits} Exploit.

entries.name: Invoke
entries: As a bonus action, you Invoke this Rune and gain resistance to all bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage for 1 minute. This effect ends early if you are {@condition Incapacitated}.
You can Invoke this Rune as part of using {@subclassFeature Runic Might|Alternate Fighter|LLAF|Runecarver|LLAF|3}.

activation.condition: In place of an attack
When a target you can see within 30 feet is hit by an attack

optionalfeature.name: Stone Rune
entries: This Rune imbues the dreamlike magic of the Stone Giants. This Rune's wielder knows the {@optfeature feint|LLExploits} Exploit.

entries.name: Invoke
entries: When a creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, can use your reaction to Invoke this Rune and force it to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, for the next minute, it is in a waking dream, has a speed of 0, and is {@condition Incapacitated}. It repeats this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending these effects on a successful save.

activation.condition: When a creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you
When a target you can see within 30 feet is hit by an attack

optionalfeature.name: Storm Rune
entries: This Rune imbues the prophetic magic and might of Storm Giants. The Rune's wielder knows the {@optfeature mighty leap|LLExploits} Exploit.

entries.name: Invoke
entries: As a bonus action, you Invoke this Rune to enter a prophetic state that lasts for 1 minute. For its duration, when a creature you can see within 60 feet makes an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, you can use your reaction to add or subtract your Exploit Die from the creature's roll.
Maintaining the prophetic state of this Rune requires your {@status concentration} as if you were concentrating on a spell.

activation.condition: When a target you can see within 30 feet is hit by an attack

optionalfeature.name: Fortifying Ration
entries: {@note Duration: 1 minute}

Upon consumption, the creature chooses either its Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution. For the duration, the creature can add your Constitution modifier (minimum of +1) to any ability check or saving throw with the chosen ability score.

optionalfeature.name: Invigorating Ration
entries: {@note Duration: instantaneous}
Upon consumption, the creature regains hit points equal to two Exploit Dice + your Constitution modifier.
At 10th level, hit points restored that exceed the target's hit point maximum become temporary hit points.

optionalfeature.name: Revitalizing Ration
entries: {@note Duration: instantaneous}

Upon consumption, the creature ends one of the following conditions afflicting it: {@condition Blinded}, {@condition Deafened}, or {@condition Poisoned}, or it can reduce its current level of {@condition Exhaustion} by 1.
At 10th level, this Ration can also remove the {@condition Charmed}, {@condition Frightened}, {@condition Paralyzed}, and {@condition Stunned} conditions.

optionalfeature.name: Stimulating Ration
entries: {@note Duration: instantaneous}
This Ration must be consumed as part of a short rest. Upon consumption, the creature gains advantage on its rolls for all Hit Dice it chooses to expend during that short rest.
At 10th level, consuming this Ration allows the creature to treat any Hit Dice it expends during that short rest as the maximum possible roll..

optionalfeature.name: Limbering Ration
entries: {@note Duration: 1 minute}
Upon consumption, the creature's speed increases by 10 feet.
At 10th level, the creature's speed increases by 20 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Thickening Ration
entries: {@note Duration: 1 minute}
Upon consumption, the creature gains resistance to either bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (its choice).
At 10th level, consuming this Ration grants it resistance to all bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

optionalfeature.name: Engorging Ration
entries: {@note Duration: 1 minute}

Upon consumption, the creature becomes Large if it was not already. For the duration, its reach increases by 5 feet, it has advantage on Strength checks and saving throws, and its melee weapon attacks it makes deal a bonus {@damage 1d4} damage.

optionalfeature.name: Heightening Ration
entries: {@note Duration: 1 minute}

Upon consumption, the creature chooses either Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. For the duration, the creature can add your Constitution modifier (minimum of +1) to ability checks and saving throws with the chosen ability score.

optionalfeature.name: Warding Ration
entries: {@note Duration: 1 minute}
Upon consumption, the creature gains resistance to one of the following: acid, cold, fire, poison, or lighting damage.
At 10th level, the creature can also choose force, necrotic, psychic, radiant, or thunder damage resistance.

optionalfeature.name: Berserker Ration
entries: {@note Duration: 1 minute}
Upon consumption, the creature does not fall {@condition unconscious} when reduced to 0 hit points. However, it still makes death saving throws as normal, and dies instantly if it fails three.

optionalfeature.name: Rejuvenating Ration
entries: {@note Duration: instant}

Upon consumption, the creature instantly gains the benefits of a short rest, including the ability to expend Hit Dice. At the end of its current turn, it gains 1 level of {@condition Exhaustion} that can only be reduced by magic or completing a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Featherweight Schematic
entries: You modify the metallurgic makeup of the object, making it significantly lighter and grating it the following benefits:

entries.name: Boots
entries: The wearer ignores the effects of mundane {@quickref difficult terrain||3} and its walking speed increases by 10 feet.

entries.name: Heavy Armor
entries: The wearer ignores penalties to {@skill Stealth} checks or Strength requirements of this armor.

entries.name: Light Armor
entries: The wearer does not take falling damage if they fall less than 100 feet, and when falling at least 10 feet, they can move horizontally 2 feet for every 1 foot they fall.

entries.name: Weapon
entries: It loses the Heavy and Two-Handed traits if it had them, and if it did not, it gains the Light and Finesse traits.

optionalfeature.name: Intuitive Schematic
entries: You modify an object so that it enhances the bearers tactical instincts in combat, granting the following benefits:

entries.name: Helm
entries: The wearer gains a bonus to any {@skill Investigation} or {@skill Perception} checks it makes equal to your Exploit Die.

entries.name: Light or Medium Armor
entries: The wearer uses its Intelligence, in place of its Dexterity for initiative rolls, and for calculating its Armor Class while wearing this armor.

entries.name: Melee Weapon
entries: The wielder uses its Intelligence, in place of its Strength or Dexterity, for both attack and damage rolls with this modified weapon.

entries.name: The Fighter
entries: The young guard quietly slipped past his comrades out the back door of the armory where the townsfolk were hiding. The bandits had come upon their village that morning, and their leader had issued a challenge. If one warrior from the town could best him in single combat, the gang would leave peacefully. Though the young man only joined the town guard one year ago, he could already best every fellow guard with the sword. To the surprise of the town, the young guard stepped out, sword drawn, ready to defend his home.

A graying dwarf made her way through the enemy encampment as the sun set. Searching for the enemy general's tent, she planned to end the battle before it began. The dwarves under her command didn't stand a chance against this great host in open combat. For what could very well be her final mission, she gripped the haft of her axe and entered the enemy general's tent.

The elven gladiator paused for a brief moment, taking in the cheering crowd. He remembered his first fight in a dark underground arena, surrounded by pirates and slavers. Now he performed for the emperor amidst the bustle of the largest metropolis in the kingdom. After savoring the moment, he whirled about and plunged his gilded spear into his foe's heart, ending his last fight, the fight that would earn him his freedom.
All three of the warriors described here are considered Fighters, true masters of the battlefield and exceptionally deadly masters with the weapons and armaments of war.

entries.name: Masters of the Battlefield
entries: Not every city guardsman, mercenary, or professional soldier is considered a true Fighter. Born with a keen battle instinct and a talent for the art of war, a true Fighter cannot resist the lure of battle. Hailing from the ranks of military officers, elite bodyguards, veteran mercenaries, and consecrated knights, Fighters are marked by their masterful skill in battle.

Delving into dungeons, slaying monsters, and other deadly work common amongst adventurers is second nature for the Fighter. Something deep within compels these warriors to seek conflict and throw themselves into it. Often champions of fair competition, Fighters make for lifelong loyal allies.

entries.name: The Armaments of War
entries: Every Fighter can swing an axe, fence with a rapier, cut down a foe with a longsword, and use a bow with a deadly degree of skill. Likewise, a Fighter is adept with shields and every form of armor. Fighters wield their weapons and armor of choice as an extension of their very self, transforming into beautiful yet deadly whirls of sharpened steel on the battlefield.

While they all have skill in combat, the nature of a Fighter's training can vary greatly. Some hone their immense physical might, crushing their foes with overwhelming blows. Others prefer to strike from afar, striking their foes down before they are aware of their presence. Still others use tactical insights to coordinate allies. And a rare few augment their techniques with a limited, but potent, esoteric battle magic.

entries.name: Creating Your Fighter
entries: When creating your Fighter, the most important thing for you to decide is how they gained their skills. Are you the scion of a noble house, trained by the best tutors that gold could buy? A gladiator who had to learn to kill or be killed? A self-made mercenary who earns food and shelter at the tip of a sword?

Also, consider how your Fighter's style of combat sets you apart from other warriors. Are you an artist with a blade who gracefully flows about the field of battle? A ruthless brigand who revels in the chaos and carnage of war? A traveling sage who only challenges those who are your equal with a sword?

entries.name: Multiclassing and the Fighter
entries: If your group uses the optional multiclassing rule, here's what you need to know when you choose to take your first level in the Fighter class.

entries.name: Ability Score Minimum
entries: As a multiclass character, you must have a minimum Strength (or Dexterity) score of 13 to take a level in this class, or to take a level in another class if you are already a Fighter.

entries.name: Proficiencies
entries: If Fighter isn't your initial class, here are the proficiencies you gain when you take your first Fighter level: light armor, medium armor, shields, simple weapons, and martial weapons.

entries.name: Exploits
entries: If you learn Exploits from more than one source, use the rules {@link Linked Here|https://www.gmbinder.com/share/-NGUL51kfZCPlESxL1wq} to determine your total number of Exploit Dice and Exploits Known.

entries.name: Quick Build
entries: You can make a Fighter quickly by using these suggestions. First, depending on the weapons you will use, make either Strength or Dexterity your highest ability score, followed by your Constitution. Second, take the {@background Soldier} background.
